markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define MemoryOntology tables/entities for schema terms, entity classes, relation classes, aliases, and stable promotion thresholds. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: MemoryOntologySchema leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryOntologySchema is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 98beb6d7; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-113 (MemoryOntologySchema) requires a Handshake-native PostgreSQL/EventLedger MemoryOntology layer defining schema terms (entity/relation classes, aliases, normalized terms, promoted extraction patterns) with a probationary->stable lifecycle gated by evidence thresholds, conflict checks, and receipt-backed promotion. The code delivers exactly this: real migration 0240 with two authority tables, lifecycle/receipt-enforcing triggers, a genuine storage layer, and integration tests proven against real managed PostgreSQL including adversarial raw-SQL negative paths.
findings: Migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0240_knowledge_memory_ontology.sql creates knowledge_memory_ontology_terms (term_kind/lifecycle_state CHECK enums, FK workspace_id->workspaces, promotion_receipt_event_id FK->kernel_event_ledger, identity UNIQUE(workspace_id,term_kind,term_key), KMO- id regex) and knowledge_memory_ontology_aliases (UNIQUE(workspace_id,alias_norm_key) so one normalized key resolves to one canonical term). Real DDL, registered in knowledge_schema_registry with authority_class='authority'.

0240 defines two enforcing triggers: trg_knowledge_memory_ontology_stable_requires_receipt (RAISE EXCEPTION if lifecycle_state='stable' AND promotion_receipt_event_id IS NULL) and trg_knowledge_memory_ontology_transition_guard (probationary->stable|retired, stable->retired, retired terminal; rejects illegal transitions). DB-layer enforcement, not app-only.

storage/knowledge_memory.rs:240-405 implements upsert_memory_ontology_term (ON CONFLICT identity upsert accruing observation_count), promote_memory_ontology_term (fails closed via is_promotable() at line 178: probationary AND (operator_approved OR observation_count>=promotion_threshold), requires EventLedger receipt id), retire_memory_ontology_term. Lines 466-535: alias add/resolve(JOIN)/list. No stubs.
Grep of storage/knowledge_memory.rs and src/knowledge_memory/*.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable returned zero hits (sub-rule 1).

tests/knowledge_memory_ontology_tests.rs runs against real managed PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg auto-discovery; appends real kernel_event_ledger events (db.append_kernel_event) as promotion receipts; includes adversarial raw-SQL negatives proving DB triggers fire: stable-without-receipt refusal (line 162-173), stable->probationary illegal-transition refusal (221-229), retired-terminal refusal (241-251), duplicate-alias-norm-key UNIQUE violation (303-317), under-threshold app+DB rejection (155-173) (sub-rule 2).

Spec 02-system-architecture.md 2.3.13.11 mandates KnowledgeClaim lifecycle (proposed/accepted/conflicted/retired) with evidence spans + resolution receipts and PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, no SQLite; the MemoryOntology term lifecycle mirrors this discipline exactly and 0241 facts back knowledge_claims rather than duplicating lifecycle. Aligned.
No forbidden-path touch: no SQLite/.db/Docker in the diff; all changes under src/backend/handshake_core and tests, within packet allowed_paths.

Aside (non-blocking): packet/anchor cites spec bundle v02.192/v02.193 while current is v02.194; same section numbers resolve and content is unchanged for this MT. Pure paperwork pointer, expected and not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
